# Work Packages: Calendar Tab

**Inputs**: Design documents from `/kitty-specs/008-calendar-tab/`
**Prerequisites**: plan.md (required), spec.md (user stories), research.md, data-model.md, quickstart.md

**Tests**: Manual testing only (per constitution — no automated tests for v1).

**Organization**: Fine-grained subtasks (`Txxx`) roll up into work packages (`WPxx`). Each work package must be independently deliverable and testable.

**Prompt Files**: Each work package references a matching prompt file in `kitty-specs/008-calendar-tab/tasks/` generated by `/spec-kitty.tasks`.

## Subtask Format: `[Txxx] [P?] Description`
- **[P]** indicates the subtask can proceed in parallel (different files/components).
- Paths relative to repository root.

---

## Work Package WP01: Foundation — ViewModel, Color Utility, View Skeleton (Priority: P0)

**Goal**: Create the CalendarViewModel, MuscleGroupColors utility, CalendarView skeleton with split-view layout, MuscleGroupDot component, and wire the calendar tab into ContentView.
**Independent Test**: Calendar tab appears in bottom nav. CalendarView renders with header, "Today" button, and split-view layout (empty content in both sections). MuscleGroupColors returns correct colors for known muscle groups.
**Prompt**: `kitty-specs/008-calendar-tab/tasks/WP01-foundation-viewmodel-skeleton.md`
**Estimated prompt size**: ~350 lines

### Included Subtasks
- [x] T001 [P] Create `Reppo/Core/Extensions/MuscleGroupColors.swift` — static color mapping for 8 muscle groups + fallback
- [x] T002 [P] Create `Reppo/Features/Calendar/Views/Components/MuscleGroupDot.swift` — colored circle indicator component
- [x] T003 Create `Reppo/Features/Calendar/ViewModels/CalendarViewModel.swift` — @Observable ViewModel with state properties, service dependencies, data loading stubs
- [x] T004 Create `Reppo/Features/Calendar/Views/CalendarView.swift` — split-view container with header, "Today" button, upper/lower ScrollView sections
- [x] T005 Wire CalendarView into `Reppo/App/ContentView.swift` — replace `CalendarPlaceholderView()` with `CalendarView`

### Implementation Notes
- T001 and T002 are independent utilities — can be done first or in parallel.
- T003 creates the ViewModel with all properties and method signatures but stub implementations. Full data loading logic comes in WP02.
- T004 creates the view skeleton using `GeometryReader` for split layout (55% calendar / 45% detail).
- T005 is a one-line replacement in ContentView.swift.
- All files follow existing MVVM patterns from Exercise and Workout features.

### Parallel Opportunities
- T001 and T002 are fully independent of each other and the ViewModel.
- T003 and T004 can be developed together (ViewModel + View).

### Dependencies
- None (starting package).

### Risks & Mitigations
- Color palette for muscle groups may need tuning on device — use easily adjustable hex values in MuscleGroupColors.swift.
- GeometryReader split layout may need percentage adjustments — keep the ratio configurable.

---

## Work Package WP02: Calendar Grid — Month Grids, Day Cells, Dots, Scrolling (Priority: P1) 🎯 MVP

**Goal**: Build the calendar month grid with vertically scrollable months, day cells showing muscle group dots, today highlighting, date selection, "Today" button scroll-to behavior, and incremental data loading for visible months.
**Independent Test**: Calendar shows scrollable month grids with correct day layouts. Dates with workouts display colored muscle group dots. Today is highlighted with blue fill. "Today" button scrolls to current date. Tapping a date visually selects it.
**Prompt**: `kitty-specs/008-calendar-tab/tasks/WP02-calendar-grid-dots-scrolling.md`
**Estimated prompt size**: ~400 lines

### Included Subtasks
- [x] T006 Create `Reppo/Features/Calendar/Views/CalendarMonthView.swift` — month header + weekday headers + 7-column LazyVGrid
- [x] T007 Create `Reppo/Features/Calendar/Views/CalendarDayCell.swift` — day number, today highlight (blue fill), selection state, muscle group dots (max 3 + overflow)
- [x] T008 Implement dot data loading in CalendarViewModel — `loadDotsForVisibleRange()` fetching Workout → WorkoutSet exerciseIds → Exercise.primaryMuscle chain
- [x] T009 Implement "Today" button scroll-to behavior via `ScrollViewReader` + `scrollTo(id:)`
- [x] T010 Implement scroll-based incremental loading — detect visible month changes, fetch new months + 1-month buffer, merge into existing dot data

### Implementation Notes
- T006: Each month is a `LazyVGrid` with 7 flexible columns. First weekday offset creates empty cells. Month/year header label above the grid. Weekday headers (S M T W T F S) as a fixed row.
- T007: Day cell shows number, blue fill circle for today, blue outline for scheduled future sessions, up to 3 colored dots + gray "+N" overflow indicator. Minimum tap target 44x44pt.
- T008: Core data loading — fetches workouts for date range, then exercise IDs per workout, then exercises (cached). Builds `[Date: [String]]` mapping. Groups workouts by date into `[Date: [Workout]]`.
- T009: Each month gets a stable ID (e.g., `"2026-02"`) for `ScrollViewReader`. "Today" button calls `scrollTo` with the current month's ID.
- T010: Use `onAppear` on month views to detect when new months become visible. Expand the loaded date range incrementally. Avoid re-fetching already-cached months.

### Parallel Opportunities
- T006 and T007 are pure view components — can be developed together.
- T008, T009, T010 depend on the ViewModel from WP01 being in place.

### Dependencies
- Depends on WP01 (ViewModel, CalendarView skeleton, MuscleGroupDot component).

### Risks & Mitigations
- Date math edge cases (month boundaries, leap years) — use `Calendar.current` APIs consistently.
- LazyVStack + LazyVGrid scrolling performance with many months — `LazyVStack` handles this well for vertical lists. Avoid loading too many months at once.
- First weekday calculation — ensure correct offset for locale (e.g., Sunday-start vs Monday-start weeks).

---

## Work Package WP03: Workout Detail — Stats, Exercise Cards, Navigation, Edge Cases (Priority: P1)

**Goal**: Build the workout detail section that appears when a date is tapped, including summary stats strip, exercise cards with read-only set rows and PR badges, navigation to ExerciseDetailView, support for multiple workouts per date, and edge case handling.
**Independent Test**: Tapping a date with a workout shows summary stats (volume, exercises, sets) and exercise cards with sets/weights/reps/PR badges in the bottom section. Tapping an exercise card navigates to ExerciseDetailView. Multiple workouts on one date show both. Empty dates show "No workout" state.
**Prompt**: `kitty-specs/008-calendar-tab/tasks/WP03-workout-detail-navigation.md`
**Estimated prompt size**: ~500 lines

### Included Subtasks
- [x] T011 [P] Create `Reppo/Features/Calendar/Views/Components/SummaryStatsStrip.swift` — horizontal strip showing volume, exercise count, set count
- [x] T012 [P] Create `Reppo/Features/Calendar/Views/Components/CalendarExerciseCard.swift` — read-only exercise card with set table, PR badges, equipment info
- [x] T013 Create `Reppo/Features/Calendar/Views/CalendarWorkoutDetailView.swift` — container orchestrating stats + exercise cards for selected date
- [x] T014 Implement workout detail loading in CalendarViewModel — `selectDate()` fetches sets, groups by exercise, computes summary stats, builds WorkoutDetail
- [x] T015 Implement navigation from exercise card to ExerciseDetailView via NavigationStack
- [x] T016 Handle multiple workouts per date — stack workouts with separator, session labels (Morning/Evening)
- [x] T017 Handle edge cases — empty state (no workout), date with no workout tap, long workout scrolling in detail area

### Implementation Notes
- T011: Follow the Summary Stat Card pattern from design-system.md Section 6.2 — row of equal-width items inside `bgCard` container, large value + tiny label, dividers between items.
- T012: Follow Exercise Card (Day View) pattern from design-system.md — header with exercise name + set count, set table rows with weight/reps/PR badge. Set rows are **read-only** (no editing from calendar). Reuse `PRBadgeView` from `Reppo/Features/Workout/Views/Components/PRBadgeView.swift`. Warmup rows at 0.45 opacity.
- T013: Receives workouts for selected date from ViewModel. Renders `SummaryStatsStrip` + `ForEach` over exercise groups → `CalendarExerciseCard`. Wraps in ScrollView for long workouts.
- T014: When user taps a date, fetch sets for each workout via `SetService.fetchSets(for: workoutId)`. Group sets by `exerciseId`, ordered by `orderInWorkout`. Compute total volume (sum of `effectiveWeight × reps`), exercise count, set count. Store as `WorkoutDetail` objects.
- T015: `CalendarExerciseCard` wrapped in `NavigationLink` or `Button` that pushes `ExerciseDetailView(exerciseId:)`. NavigationStack is already in CalendarView from WP01.
- T016: `ForEach` over workouts for the date. If >1 workout, show session label (derive from `startTime`: "Morning Session" / "Evening Session" / "Session 1, 2..."). Visual divider between workout sections.
- T017: Empty state when no workout — centered text "No workout" in `textTertiary`. If no date selected, show instruction text. Workout detail ScrollView ensures long workouts scroll properly.

### Parallel Opportunities
- T011 and T012 are independent view components — can be developed together.
- T013 depends on T011 and T012 (composes them).
- T014 depends on the ViewModel structure from WP01.

### Dependencies
- Depends on WP02 (calendar grid with date selection must work before detail can be shown).

### Risks & Mitigations
- Volume display formatting — use user's unit preference for weight display (kg/lbs). Constitution says store metric, convert in UI only.
- ExerciseDetailView integration — already confirmed reusable from feature 007 (takes `exerciseId: UUID`).
- Performance with many exercises per workout — use lazy loading in ScrollView. Typical workout has 3-6 exercises.

---

## Dependency & Execution Summary

- **Sequence**: WP01 → WP02 → WP03
- **Parallelization**: Within each WP, subtasks marked `[P]` can be done simultaneously (different files). WP02 and WP03 are sequential because the detail view depends on date selection from the calendar grid.
- **MVP Scope**: WP01 + WP02 constitute the minimum visual calendar. WP03 adds the workout detail that makes it functional.
